notes: HDMI version 1.4 confirmed by multiple retailer spec tables (projectorcentral, imageprointernational). The single VGA port labeled 'Computer' is switchable between input and monitor-out via menu; it is physically one D-Sub 15-pin connector. Audio inputs are 2x 3.5mm stereo mini-jack, confirmed by Epson product page description ('stereo mini'); prior entry incorrectly listed them as RCA connectors. LAN speed grade (eth-100m) is inferred — not stated in specs. Power inlet type (IEC C13) is inferred from standard projector design; Epson publishes only voltage range. USB grades (usb-2.0) are inferred for this 2018-era projector. HDBaseT RJ45 is the video/control input port; the separate LAN RJ45 is for network control only.

ports.name: HDMI 1
ports.note: HDMI 1.4; no MHL on this port

ports.name: Computer
ports.note: D-Sub 15-pin; configurable as VGA input or Monitor Out (daisy chain output) via menu setting

ports.name: HDBaseT
ports.note: RJ45 jack; carries video, audio, RS-232C, and power over Cat cable

ports.name: LAN
ports.note: Wired LAN for network control; speed grade inferred (100M typical for this era projector)

ports.name: USB-A
ports.note: USB Type-A; used for USB memory/firmware/wireless adapter

ports.name: USB-B
ports.note: USB Type-B; used for USB display connection from computer

ports.name: Audio In 1
ports.note: 3.5mm stereo mini-jack audio input; one of two stereo audio inputs

ports.name: Audio In 2
ports.note: 3.5mm stereo mini-jack audio input; one of two stereo audio inputs

ports.name: AC In
ports.note: IEC C13 power inlet inferred — standard for this projector class; Epson specs only state 100–240V AC, connector type not explicitly published
